nodes.gloss: Initially, the body is bathed, dressed in new clothes, and placed on a wooden pyre. Holy water (Ganga Jal) and a Tulsi leaf are put in the mouth.

nodes.label: Visarjana

nodes.label: Kalasha Sthapana
nodes.gloss: The family members carefully collect the deceased's bones and ashes following the cremation. These remains are placed in a Kalash, or urn, which is usually made of metal or clay. A scarlet fabric is put around the urn to conceal the opening.

nodes.label: Pushpa
nodes.gloss: The family of the deceased honours them by offering water with sesame seeds and flowers, showing gratitude and seeking their blessings.

nodes.label: Namaskara
nodes.gloss: After performing the Puja as mentioned above, all the children, men and women present during the Puja should hold flowers in their hands and chant the praises of Lord Ganesha , Maha Lakshmi , Maha Kali , Maha Saraswati and Kubera and bow down to them while offering flowers to them in the order of the young to old.

nodes.label: Katha / Path
nodes.gloss: A Sacred shanti mantra is recited by the pandit to cleanse the environment and seek the blessings of Lord Vishnu and pitrus.

nodes.label: Aarti

nodes.label: Sankalpa
nodes.gloss: Begin the puja by performing Sankalp — a formal declaration of your intention to honor your ancestors. Announce the names and gotra of the departed souls you wish to remember.

nodes.label: Avahana
nodes.gloss: Invoke the ancestral deities (Pitru Devatas) with proper mantras. Since Bharani Nakshatra is ruled by Lord Yama, special prayers are offered to him for ensuring smooth passage of the departed soul.

nodes.label: Tambula
nodes.gloss: Conclude the ceremony by offering food (Brahmin Bhojan) to a qualified Pandit along with Dakshina (offering/fee). This act completes the ritual and ensures the blessings reach the departed soul.

nodes.label: Aarti
nodes.gloss: Offer the final prayers (Pitru Shanti Prarthana) seeking peace and liberation for ancestors and well-being for the entire family. Close the puja with Aarti.

nodes.label: Havan / Homa
nodes.gloss: Optionally, perform a Thila Homam (fire ritual using black sesame seeds) to multiply the spiritual benefit of the Shraddha ceremony and resolve Pitru Dosha.

nodes.label: Tambula
nodes.gloss: When the bhoj finished, offer them Dakshina, fruits, and betel leaves as a symbol of gratitude.

nodes.label: Muhurat selection
nodes.gloss: It is very significant to use correct Yantra for Puja. Without correct Yantra, the purpose of Yantra worship would not be fulfilled. Yantra Puja needs Muhurat and should be done on an auspicious day and time. For Shri Sukta Yantra Puja, the days of Lakshmi Puja , Dhanteras and Pushya Nakshatra are considered auspicious.

nodes.label: Shuddhi / Cleaning
nodes.gloss: The puja then performs Achamana (sipping ritual of water) and Pranayama to cleanse the body and mind. The devotee then makes a sankalpa – a solemn declaration of their name, gotra, and the particular intention for which the ten Mahavidyas are being performed.

nodes.label: Sankalpa

nodes.label: Kalasha Sthapana
nodes.gloss: The ten yantras represent each Mahavidya and are स्थापित in a prepared puja space. Each yantra is then energized with specific beej mantras.

nodes.label: Vastra
nodes.gloss: A young girl, seen as the living form of Devi, is worshipped with flowers, clothes, and sweets. The ritual concludes with feeding Brahmins and distributing prasad to all devotees.

nodes.label: Gandha
nodes.gloss: After Peetha Puja, Avarana Puja should begin. Avarana Puja is the most important part of Yantra Puja. For Shri Sukta Yantra, total nine Avarana Puja are performed. During each Avarana Puja, Yantra should be worshiped with Akshata, flower, Dhoop, Deep and Gandha while chanting each Mantra. Tarpan should be done along with each Mantra.

nodes.label: Pushpa
nodes.gloss: Each of the ten Mahavidyas is worshipped through this ritual, offering water, flowers, incense, light, food, and prayers to the goddess.

nodes.label: Dhupa

nodes.label: Havan / Homa

nodes.label: Sankalpa
nodes.gloss: After performing Achamana, clean your right hand palm by taking water into it through five-vessel. Take fresh water, Akshata, flowers etc. into cleaned right hand palm and read Puja Sankalpa Mantra.

nodes.label: Dhyana
nodes.gloss: After the self-purification, begin Puja with the meditation of Lord Dhanvantari. Dhyana should be done in already ignited Ghee lamp flame kept in front of you.

nodes.label: Avahana
nodes.gloss: After Dhyana of Shri Dhanvantari Devata, one should chant following Mantra in front of ignited Ghee lamp flame, by showing Avahan Mudra (Avahan Mudra is formed by joining both palms and folding both thumbs inwards).

nodes.label: Padya
nodes.gloss: After welcoming Shri Dhanvantari offer Him water to wash the feet while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Arghya
nodes.gloss: After Padya offering, offer water to Shri Dhanvantari for head Abhishekam while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Achamana
nodes.gloss: Take clean water in right hand by five-vessel for three times to offer Achamana.

nodes.label: Vastra
nodes.gloss: Keep a wooden chowki, cover it with red cloth, and place an idol or images of Lord Ganesha, Goddess Lakshmi, and Lord Kubera. Place your newly purchased jewellery on the altar to be blessed.

nodes.label: Gandha
nodes.gloss: Offer Chandan to Shri Dhanvantari while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Pushpa
nodes.gloss: After Shri Dhanvantari has been invoked, take five flowers in Anjali (by joining palm of both hands) and leave them in front of the you to offer seat to Shri Dhanvantari while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Dhupa
nodes.gloss: Offer Dhoop to Shri Dhanvantari while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Naivedya
nodes.gloss: Offer Naivedya to Shri Dhanvantari while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Tambula
nodes.gloss: Now offer Tambula (Paan with betel nuts) to Shri Dhanvantari while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Namaskara
nodes.gloss: Now offer symbolic Pradakshina (circumambulate from left to right of Shri Dhanvantari) with flowers while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Aarti
nodes.gloss: Purify the puja space thoroughly and decorate the entrance with flowers and rangolis or footprints of Goddess Lakshmi to greet her.

nodes.label: Mantra Pushpanjali
nodes.gloss: After Shri Dhanvantari has been invoked, take five flowers in Anjali (by joining palm of both hands) and leave them in front of the you to offer seat to Shri Dhanvantari while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Kshama Prarthana
nodes.gloss: Now seek pardon from Shri Dhanvantari for any known-unknown mistakes done during Puja while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Avahana
nodes.gloss: Puja should begin with the meditation and invocation of Goddess Durga, one should chant following Mantra in front of Devi Durga Murti, by showing Avahan Mudra (Avahana Mudra is formed by joining both palms and folding both thumbs inwards).

nodes.label: Arghya

nodes.label: Achamana

nodes.label: Vastra

nodes.label: Yajnopavita

nodes.label: Gandha
nodes.gloss: After Abhushana offering, offer Chandan to Goddess Durga while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Pushpa

nodes.label: Dhupa

nodes.label: Naivedya

nodes.label: Tambula

nodes.label: Namaskara

nodes.label: Katha / Path
nodes.gloss: This day signifies the beginning of Devi Paksha, the fortnight dedicated to the Goddess. Devotees chant the sacred verses of Chandi Path to awaken the Goddess. It is also the time to perform Tarpan , an offering to ancestors.

nodes.label: Aarti
nodes.gloss: Now perform Goddess Durga Aarti after chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Mantra Pushpanjali
nodes.gloss: Now offer Pushpanjali to Goddess Durga while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Kshama Prarthana

nodes.label: Visarjana
nodes.gloss: To complete the ritual, married women on the last day offer vermilion to the Goddess, followed by the immersion of the idol, depicting the return of the Divine to the formless eternal.

nodes.label: Havan / Homa
nodes.gloss: पूर्व दिशा की ओर मुख करके बैठ जायें तथा अपने सामने हवन कुण्ड रखें। पृथ्वी माता का ध्यान करें और अपना भार उठाने हेतु आभार व्यक्त करें। अपनी हथेली स्वच्छ करें तथा उसमें तीन बार जल हथेली में लेकर निम्नलिखित मन्त्रों का उच्चारण करते हुये आचमन करें: अब एक दीपक प्रज्वलित करें एवं अग्निदेव का आवाहन करें। आप प्राणायाम भी कर सकते हैं अथवा साधारण रूप से मस्तिष्क के शान्त होने तक लम्बी श्वास लेते रहें। अब अपने दायें हाथ में जल लेकर निम्नलिखित मन्त्र का उच्चारण करें- यह बोलते हुये जल को अपने सामने भूमि पर छोड़ दें। हवन कुण्ड में कुछ सूखे नारियल के टुकड़े, लकड़ी अथवा उपले डालें तथा मचीस और कपूर की सहायता से निम्नलिखित मन्त्र का उच्चारण करते हुये अग्नि प्रज्वलित करें। निम्नलिखित मन्त्र का उच्चारण करते हुये अग्नि में आठ बार घी डालें तथा ऐसी कल्पना करें कि अग्नि पवित्र एवं शक्तिशाली होती जा रही है।

nodes.label: Vastra
nodes.gloss: Now offer Moli (मोली) as new clothes to Lord Ganesha while chanting following Mantra. After Vastra Samarpan, now offer clothes for upper body parts (Uttariya) to Lord Ganesha while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Yajnopavita
nodes.gloss: After Vastra offering, offer Yajnopavita to Lord Ganesha while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Gandha
nodes.gloss: After Yajnopavita offering, offer scent to Lord Ganesha while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Pushpa
nodes.gloss: Now offer garland made of flowers to Lord Ganesha, then offer Shami Patra, then Durva with three or five leaflets, and then vermilion (Sindoor) for Tilak, each while chanting the respective Mantra.

nodes.label: Dhupa
nodes.gloss: Now offer Dhoop to Lord Ganesha while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Dipa
nodes.gloss: Now offer Dipa to Lord Ganesha while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Naivedya
nodes.gloss: Now offer Naivedya to Lord Ganesha while chanting following Mantra. After Naivedya offering, offer Chandana mixed with water to Lord Ganesha (Karodvartana) while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Tambula
nodes.gloss: Now offer Tambula (Paan with betel nuts) to Lord Ganesha, then Narikela (coconut), then Dakshina (gift), each while chanting the respective Mantra.

nodes.label: Aarti
nodes.gloss: Ganesh Aarti is performed, followed by the offering of Modak, laddoo, coconut, fruits, and other prasad.

nodes.label: Visarjana
nodes.gloss: After Tambula Arpana and Dakshina Samarpana, perform Lord Ganesha Aarti (Nirajana). Then offer Pushpanjali, perform symbolic Pradakshina (circumambulate from left to right of Shri Ganesha) with flowers, and conclude the Puja with Visarjana, each while chanting the respective Mantra.

nodes.label: Achamana (self-purification)
nodes.gloss: Aachaman (आचमन).

nodes.label: Ganesha Puja / Smarana
nodes.gloss: After seeking pardon from Shri Ganesha, chant following Mantra while requesting to Shri Ganesha to protect yourself, after Shodashopachara Puja.

nodes.label: Kalasha Sthapana
nodes.gloss: Place the kalash filled with water near the puja altar. Decorate the kalash with mango leaves and a coconut tied with a red cloth.

nodes.label: Dhyana
nodes.gloss: Puja should begin with the meditation of Lord Ganesha. Dhyana should be done in front of already installed Shri Ganesha statue in front of you. Following Mantra should be chanted while meditating on Lord Ganesha.

nodes.label: Avahana
nodes.gloss: Avahana (Invocation): Invoke the lord ganesh by chanting 'Om Gam Ganapataye Namah', invite his divine energy into the idol.

nodes.label: Asana
nodes.gloss: Aasan Samarpan (आसन समर्पण) - offering seat to Lord Ganesha.

nodes.label: Padya
nodes.gloss: After welcoming Shri Ganesha offer Him water to wash the feet while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Arghya
nodes.gloss: After Padya offering, offer water to Shri Ganesha for head Abhishekam while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Achamana
nodes.gloss: Now offer water to Shri Ganesha for Achamana while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Snana
nodes.gloss: Abhishek & Snanam: Sprinkle Panchamrit (a mix of honey, curd, ghee, sugar, and milk) on the idol as a symbol of ritual bath.

nodes.label: Vastra
nodes.gloss: Vastra Samarpan (वस्त्र समर्पण एवं उत्तरीय समर्पण).

nodes.label: Gandha
nodes.gloss: Offerings (Arpan): Apply a Sandalwood paste (Chandan) and Kumkum tilak to the lord. Present Durva Grass (group of three or twenty-one blades) and red flowers. Put a sacred thread (Janeu) or a piece of red cloth over the deity's photo.

nodes.label: Pushpa
nodes.gloss: Aarti & Pushpanjali: Complete the puja with the final circular worship with a camphor flame while singing the Ganesh Aarti. Offer a handful of flowers as a gesture of total surrender.

nodes.label: Dhupa
nodes.gloss: Now offer Dhoop to Shri Ganesha while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Dipa
nodes.gloss: Deep Prajwalana: Light a ghee lamp to cleanse the environment and witness the puja.

nodes.label: Naivedya
nodes.gloss: Naivedya (Bhog): Present sweets, especially Modaks or Laddoos, including fresh seasonal fruits and betel leaves.

nodes.label: Tambula
nodes.gloss: Now offer Tambula (Paan with betel nuts) to Shri Ganesha while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Namaskara
nodes.gloss: Now offer symbolic Pradakshina (circumambulate from left to right of Shri Ganesha) with flowers while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Aarti
nodes.gloss: Preparation: Purify the puja place and yourself. Keep the lord idol on a wooden chowki covered with a red cloth.

nodes.label: Mantra Pushpanjali
nodes.gloss: Aarti & Pushpanjali: Complete the puja with the final circular worship with a camphor flame while singing the Ganesh Aarti. Offer a handful of flowers as a gesture of total surrender.

nodes.label: Kshama Prarthana
nodes.gloss: Now seek pardon from Shri Ganesha for any known-unknown mistakes done during Puja while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Prasad Distribution
nodes.gloss: Distribute the prasad (food offering) among family members and devotees as a token of blessings from Lord Ganesh.

nodes.title: Shri Ganapati Prana Pratishta Mantra (Sankshipta Vidhi)
Shri Ganapati Dashopachara Pujan Mantra (Sankshipta Vidhi)

nodes.label: Sankalpa
nodes.gloss: The family takes a formal vow, showing the merit of reading to the ancestors to assure their smooth journey into the ancestral realm.

nodes.label: Dipa
nodes.gloss: The path starts with preparing a small wooden pedestal where the sacred scripture is kept. A lamp is lit to show the divine existence of lord vishnu.

nodes.label: Katha / Path
nodes.gloss: A certified pandit chants the chapters daily, often in the afternoon. The family sits together, as hearing the path is believed meritorious for both the living and the dead.

nodes.label: Shuddhi / Cleaning
nodes.gloss: Clean the space and sprinkle Gangajal to purify the house. Honour the broom with Haldi-Kumkum. Take a bath to complete self-purification (Atma Shodhan) and make Sankalpana by taking water in your fist.

nodes.label: Achamana (self-purification)
nodes.gloss: Aachaman (आचमन).

nodes.label: Ganesha Puja / Smarana
nodes.gloss: Before the main puja, worship Lord Ganesha (Vighnaharta), Lord Kubera, and the three forms of Goddess Lakshmi - Mahasaraswati, Mahalakshmi and Mahakali.

nodes.label: Kalasha Sthapana
nodes.gloss: Spread rice grains in the centre of the chowki as an Asana. Apply Haldi-Kumkum to the Kalash and idols. Fill the silver or brass Kalash with water, supari, coins, Akshat rice, marigold flower and a pinch of Haldi-Kumkum, and cover the mouth with mango leaves and a coconut.

nodes.label: Dhyana
nodes.gloss: Recite Ganesh mantras and stotras to invoke the divine presence and seek blessings. Meditate on Lord Ganesh, focusing on his form and attributes.

nodes.label: Avahana
nodes.gloss: Puja begins with invocation of Lord Ganesha (Avahanam) showing the Avahan Mudra, followed by Pratishthapanam - installing Lord Ganesha into the statue. (Skipped for a daily-worshipped pre-installed statue.)

nodes.label: Asana
nodes.gloss: Take five flowers in Anjali and leave them in front of the Murti to offer a seat to Shri Ganesha.

nodes.label: Padya
nodes.gloss: Offer water to Lord Ganesha to wash the feet.

nodes.label: Arghya
nodes.gloss: Offer scented water to Lord Ganesha.

nodes.label: Achamana
nodes.gloss: Offer water to Lord Ganesha for Achamana.

nodes.label: Snana
nodes.gloss: Offer water for the bath, followed by Panchamrita Snana and baths with milk (Paya/Dugdha), curd (Dadhi), ghee (Ghrita), honey (Madhu), sugar (Sharkara), scented oil (Suvasita) and pure Gangajal (Shuddhodaka).

nodes.label: Vastra
nodes.gloss: Offer Moli as new clothes (Vastra), then offer clothes for the upper body (Uttariya).

nodes.label: Yajnopavita
nodes.gloss: Offer Yajnopavita (sacred thread) to Lord Ganesha.

nodes.label: Gandha
nodes.gloss: Offer scent (Chandan/sandalwood) to Lord Ganesha.

nodes.label: Pushpa
nodes.gloss: Offer a flower garland (Pushpa Mala), Shami Patra, Durva with three or five leaflets (Durvankura), and vermilion (Sindoor) for Tilak.

nodes.label: Dhupa
nodes.gloss: Offer Dhoop (incense) to Lord Ganesha.

nodes.label: Dipa
nodes.gloss: Offer Dipa (lamp) to Lord Ganesha.

nodes.label: Naivedya
nodes.gloss: Offer Naivedya (food, including Modak and fruits), then offer Chandana mixed with water (Karodvartana).

nodes.label: Tambula
nodes.gloss: Offer Tambula (Paan with betel nuts), Narikela (coconut) and Dakshina (gift) to Lord Ganesha.

nodes.label: Aarti
nodes.gloss: Purify the puja place and yourself. Keep the lord idol on a wooden chowki covered with a red cloth.

nodes.label: Mantra Pushpanjali
nodes.gloss: Complete the puja with the final circular worship with a camphor flame while singing the Ganesh Aarti. Offer a handful of flowers as a gesture of total surrender.

nodes.label: Visarjana
nodes.gloss: Perform Aarti (Nirajana), offer Pushpanjali, do symbolic Pradakshina, and conclude with Visarjana while chanting the closing (kshama) mantra.

nodes.label: Prasad Distribution
nodes.gloss: Distribute the prasad (food offering) among family members and devotees as a token of blessings from Lord Ganesh.

nodes.label: Shuddhi / Cleaning
nodes.gloss: Take some water in your right hand and sip it in small proportions three times, chanting Om Narayanaya Namaha or Om Keshavaya Namaha. It helps in cleaning the mind, body, and soul.

nodes.label: Sankalpa
nodes.gloss: Close your eyes and state the purpose and duration of the Jaap.

nodes.label: Ganesha Puja / Smarana
nodes.gloss: Before the Jaap, offer prayers to Lord Ganesha to ensure completion of the ritual without any obstacles.

nodes.label: Pushpa
nodes.gloss: Use a Tulsi or Rudraksha mala (108 beads or more) for chanting. Hold a mala in your right hand and use your thumb and middle finger to move the beads while chanting a mantra with correct pronunciation.

nodes.label: Aarti
nodes.gloss: Take a holy bath and wear traditional yet comfortable clothes. Arrange the puja setup facing East and place the idol or image of Goddess Gayatri and Lord Ganesha on the wooden chowki.

nodes.label: Kshama Prarthana
nodes.gloss: A final prayer is made in order to seek forgiveness for any unintentional mistake during the Jaap.

nodes.label: Havan / Homa
nodes.gloss: The ritual concludes with a sacred fire ceremony where offerings of ghee and sacred herbs are offered to Agni Dev to seal the positivity.

nodes.label: Shuddhi / Cleaning
nodes.gloss: It is a ceremony of the purification of the mother using five sacred substances-milk, honey, ghee, curd and sugar to ensure positive cosmic energy favouring the baby.

nodes.label: Ganesha Puja / Smarana
nodes.gloss: We start by invoking Lord Ganesha to eliminate all the odds and goddess Gauri to give the mother the power of the Universal Mother.

nodes.label: Kalasha Sthapana
nodes.gloss: The fabricated image of Mount Govardhan is made by the use of cow dung and adorned with flowers, lamps, and colours.

nodes.label: Snana
nodes.gloss: A religious bath is given to the Shila using such things as milk, curd, and honey by reciting a Vedic mantra to draw the gods.

nodes.label: Pushpa
nodes.gloss: Cows are washed and decorated with garlands. Special offerings and prayers are made in their honor, recognizing the incarnation of Mother Earth.

nodes.label: Namaskara
nodes.gloss: Devotees go around the Govardhan hills singing bhajans and hymns to reduce the sins of the past and as a sign of devotion.

nodes.label: Aarti
nodes.gloss: Flowers, incense stick, Annakut, and lamp are presented to the image of Mount Govardhan. A maha aarti is performed while singing devotional songs.

nodes.label: Prasad Distribution
nodes.gloss: To conclude the puja, an annakut in the form of prasad is distributed among all the family members, friends, neighbours, and the needy people.

nodes.label: Muhurat selection
nodes.gloss: The puja should be completed on a "Shubh Muhurat" (auspicious time) estimated based on the Hindu Lunar Calendar (Panchang).

nodes.label: Shuddhi / Cleaning
nodes.gloss: Before the Griha Pravesh Puja can commence, it is essential to thoroughly clean and purify the new home; the house should be swept, mopped, and dusted, with special attention to the puja room, and it is customary to use holy water or 'Gangajal' for purification purposes.

nodes.label: Ganesha Puja / Smarana
nodes.gloss: Lord Ganesha, the remover of hindrances, is pleased first. This is observed by a Vastu Puja to honour the spirits of the land.

nodes.label: Navagraha / attendant deities
nodes.gloss: To appease the nine planets and remove any astrological obstacles.

nodes.label: Avahana
nodes.gloss: The recitation of mantras is a pivotal aspect of the Griha Pravesh Puja; these sacred chants are believed to purify the atmosphere and invoke the presence of divine energies, typically accompanied by the ringing of bells and the aroma of incense, with family members repeating the mantras after the priest.

nodes.label: Yajnopavita
nodes.gloss: Many families decorate the vessel with Kumkum, Turmeric, Mango leaves, and Sacred thread.

nodes.label: Pushpa
nodes.gloss: During the Griha Pravesh Puja, offerings to the deities are of paramount importance; offerings typically include fresh flowers, fruits, sweets, incense sticks, and betel leaves and nuts, with specific mantras chanted while presenting these offerings.

nodes.label: Dhupa
nodes.gloss: The recitation of mantras is a pivotal aspect of the Griha Pravesh Puja; these sacred chants are believed to purify the atmosphere and invoke the presence of divine energies, typically accompanied by the ringing of bells and the aroma of incense, with family members repeating the mantras after the priest.

nodes.label: Naivedya
nodes.gloss: Offer the prepared milk to the deity before serving anyone.

nodes.label: Tambula
nodes.gloss: Once the puja area is set up, it is time to invite the priest, who will lead the Griha Pravesh Puja, guiding the homeowners through the various rituals and mantras; it is customary to invite the priest well in advance and discuss the puja schedule, the list of samagri required, and the dakshina to be offered.

nodes.label: Aarti
nodes.gloss: Clean the entire home with salt water.

nodes.label: Dwar Puja (threshold)
nodes.gloss: The owner of the house enters carrying a Kalash (copper pot) filled with water, a coconut, and mango leaves. The hall is decorated with Rangoli and a Toran of marigolds.

nodes.label: Vastu Shanti / Homa
nodes.gloss: Lord Ganesha, the remover of hindrances, is pleased first. This is observed by a Vastu Puja to honour the spirits of the land.

nodes.label: Milk Boiling Ceremony
nodes.gloss: An important ritual where milk is boiled until it overflows. This shows an abundance of wealth and food in the new household.

nodes.label: Havan / Homa
nodes.gloss: A fire sacrifice is conducted to cleanse the atmosphere. The smoke from the Havan is supposed to remove evil spirits and negative energies.

nodes.label: Gau Puja (cow)
nodes.gloss: Worshipping the cow (Kamadhenu).

nodes.label: Sankalpa
nodes.gloss: Lord Hanuman Puja should begin with the Sankalpa. For that one should clean the right hand palm by taking water into it through five-vessel (पञ्च-पात्र). After that one should take fresh water, Akshata, flowers etc. into cleaned right hand palm and read following Sankalpa Mantra. After reading Sankalpa Mantra water should be dropped at the ground.

nodes.label: Ganesha Puja / Smarana
nodes.gloss: Start the path with a small prayer to Lord Ganesh, followed by a prayer to Lord Rama. Read the Chalisa with full dedication and focus.

nodes.label: Dhyana
nodes.gloss: Dhyana should be done in front of already installed Lord Hanuman statue in front of you. Following Mantra should be chanted while meditating on Lord Hanuman.

nodes.label: Avahana
nodes.gloss: After taking Sankalpa, one should chant following Mantra in front of the Murti, by showing Avahan Mudra (Avahan Mudra is formed by joining both palms and folding both thumbs inwards).

nodes.label: Asana
nodes.gloss: After meditation of Lord Hanuman, take five flowers in Anjali (by joining palm of both hands) and leave them in front of the Murti to offer seat to Lord Hanuman while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Padya
nodes.gloss: After offering seat to Lord Hanuman offer Him water to wash the feet while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Arghya
nodes.gloss: After Padya offering, offer water to Lord Hanuman for head Abhishekam while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Achamana
nodes.gloss: After Arghya offering, offer water to Lord Hanuman for Achamana (water for sipping) while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Snana
nodes.gloss: Panchamrita Snanam (पञ्चामृत-स्नानम्) After Achamana, now give a bath with Panchamrita (the mixture of milk, curd, honey, Ghee and sugar) to Lord Hanuman while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Vastra
nodes.gloss: After Katisutra and Kaupina offering, now offer clothes for upper body parts to Lord Hanuman while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Yajnopavita
nodes.gloss: After Uttariya offering, offer Yajnopavita to Lord Hanuman while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Gandha
nodes.gloss: After Yajnopavita offering, offer scent to Lord Hanuman while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Pushpa
nodes.gloss: After Akshata offering, offer flowers to Lord Hanuman while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Dhupa
nodes.gloss: Now offer Dhupa to Lord Hanuman while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Dipa
nodes.gloss: Now offer Deep to Lord Hanuman while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Naivedya
nodes.gloss: Now offer Naivedya to Lord Hanuman while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Tambula
nodes.gloss: Now offer Tambula (Paan with betel nuts) to Lord Hanuman while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Namaskara
nodes.gloss: Now offer symbolic Pradakshina (circumambulate from left to right of Lord Hanuman) with flowers while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Katha / Path
nodes.gloss: Reading a single path is beneficial; some prefer to chant it 7, 11, or 100 times to embody specific purposes and gain mental clarity.

nodes.label: Aarti
nodes.gloss: After Tambula Samarpan, perform Lord Hanuman Aarti after chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Mantra Pushpanjali
nodes.gloss: Now offer Pushpanjali to Lord Hanuman while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.gloss: After Katisutra and Kaupina offering, now offer clothes for upper body parts to Lord Hanuman while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.gloss: After Uttariya offering, offer Yajnopavita to Lord Hanuman while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.gloss: Now offer Dhupa to Lord Hanuman while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Dipa
nodes.gloss: Now offer Deep to Lord Hanuman while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Naivedya
nodes.gloss: Now offer Naivedya to Lord Hanuman while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Tambula
nodes.gloss: Now offer Tambula (Paan with betel nuts) to Lord Hanuman while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Namaskara
nodes.gloss: Now offer symbolic Pradakshina (circumambulate from left to right of Lord Hanuman) with flowers while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Katha / Path
nodes.gloss: Now, the Hanuman Chalisa and Sunderkand path are chanted to invoke Lord Hanuman’s sacred grace.

nodes.label: Aarti
nodes.gloss: After Tambula Samarpan, perform Lord Hanuman Aarti after chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Mantra Pushpanjali
nodes.gloss: Now offer Pushpanjali to Lord Hanuman while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Shuddhi / Cleaning
nodes.gloss: Keep all Puja ingredients in a plate. Accompany a small water pot with Puja Thali. While on the Puja spot, sit down such that either facing the East or the North direction. After that sprinkle some water on the Puja Thali and yourself while chanting following Mantra thrice.

nodes.label: Sankalpa
nodes.gloss: At the place of puja, a sacred material or a symbolic model is created, representing Holika. Now devotees take a vow to perform the ritual with a pure heart.

nodes.label: Ganesha Puja / Smarana
nodes.gloss: Now remember Lord Ganesha after taking flower and rice in the right hand. While reciting the Mantra, apply Roli and rice on a flower and offer it along with fragrance to Lord Ganesha.

nodes.label: Kalasha Sthapana
nodes.gloss: At the place of puja, a sacred material or a symbolic model is created, representing Holika. Now devotees take a vow to perform the ritual with a pure heart.

nodes.label: Gandha
nodes.gloss: Now remember Lord Narasimha by chanting following Mantra. While reciting below Mantra, applying Roli and rice on a flower and offer it along with fragrance to Lord Narasimha.

nodes.label: Namaskara
nodes.gloss: A fire is set in the auspicious muhurat. Devotess circles the fire (Parikrama) and does prayers.

nodes.label: Sankalpa
nodes.gloss: The individual begins the ritual by taking a formal vow to complete it with devotion, naming themselves and their birth details.

nodes.label: Navagraha / attendant deities
nodes.gloss: Before performing rituals for the Dosh, prayers are offered to Lord Ganesha to eliminate obstacles and to the nine planets for overall harmony.

nodes.label: Snana
nodes.gloss: Silver idols of Rahu and Ketu (represented as Nagas) are offered with abhishekam in milk and honey.

nodes.label: Aarti
nodes.gloss: Before the Kaal Sarp Dosh Pooja, participants and performers should take a bath.

nodes.label: Visarjana
nodes.gloss: The ritual concludes by immersing the energised idols in a holy river, symbolising the flowing away of all life's obstructions.

nodes.label: Havan / Homa
nodes.gloss: The Navagrahas immediately began to perform Havan after their worship and adoration of Lord Shiva on the Kalash (mixed with Ghee and Kala).

nodes.label: Dhyana
nodes.gloss: Puja should begin with the meditation of Bhagawati Kali. Dhyana should be done in front of Lekhani-Dawat symbolically representing Bhagawati Kali. Following Mantra should be chanted while meditating on Bhagawati Kali.

nodes.label: Avahana
nodes.gloss: After Dhyanam of Bhagwati Kali, invoke Her in front of Lekhani-Dawat with following Mantra. While calling Bhagwati Kali your hands should be in Avahan Mudra in front of Lekhani-Dawat (Avahan Mudra is formed by joining both palms and folding both thumbs inwards).

nodes.label: Gandha
nodes.gloss: After this perform Bhagwati Kali Puja with Chandan, Akshata, Pushpa, Dhoop, Deep and Naivedhya while chanting following Mantras.

nodes.label: Pushpa
nodes.gloss: Once Bhagwati Kali has come after invoking, take five flowers in Anjali (by joining palm of both hands) and leave them in front of Lekhani-Dawat to offer the seat to Bhagwati Kali while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Mantra Pushpanjali
nodes.gloss: Once Bhagwati Kali has come after invoking, take five flowers in Anjali (by joining palm of both hands) and leave them in front of Lekhani-Dawat to offer the seat to Bhagwati Kali while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Muhurat selection
nodes.gloss: Book a pandit to fix the ritual date depending on the child’s birth nakshatra. Odd months like 3rd, 5th, and 7th, and bright fortnights are traditionally advised. Prevent Ashtami, Navami, and Amavasya tithi to plan.

nodes.label: Sankalpa
nodes.gloss: The puja starts with invoking Lord Ganesha to eliminate problems, followed by sankalpa. The father formally mentions the name of the child, the gotra, and the purpose in the presence of the priest.

nodes.label: Ganesha Puja / Smarana
nodes.gloss: The puja starts with invoking Lord Ganesha to eliminate problems, followed by sankalpa. The father formally mentions the name of the child, the gotra, and the purpose in the presence of the priest.

nodes.label: Aarti
nodes.gloss: To pierce the right ear of male children first and the left ear of female children, a gold or silver needle is used as per traditional Vedic preference. Particular protective mantras are recited continuously during the ceremony to assure the comfort and divine guardianship for the child.

nodes.label: Havan / Homa
nodes.gloss: A short Ayush homam is conducted, presenting ghee and samagri into the sacred fire while reciting longevity mantras for the child. The ritual is completed with Arti, family blessings, and the sharing of prasad to all.

nodes.label: Sankalpa
nodes.gloss: On the day of fasting, after taking morning bath women should take the pledge, which is called Sankalp, to keep the fast for the wellbeing of the husband and the family. It is also recited during Sankalp that the fasting would be without any food or the water and the fast would be broken after sighting the moon.

nodes.label: Ganesha Puja / Smarana
nodes.gloss: According to Vratraj, the best time to perform Karwa Chauth Puja is during Sandhya time which starts after sunset. Karwa Chauth Puja is focused to seek blessing of Goddess Parvati. Women either draw Goddess Gaura and Chauth Mata on the wall or use Chauth Mata image on the printed Karwa Chauth Puja Calendar to worship Mata Parvati. Goddess Gaura and Chauth Mata are representations of Goddess Parvati. After Goddess Gaura, Lord Shiva, Lord Kartikeya and Lord Ganesha are worshipped. Usually women perform Puja in a group and narrate the Story of Karwa Chauth Mahatamya, known as magnanimous of Karwa Chauth fasting.

nodes.label: Shuddhi / Cleaning
nodes.gloss: Purify the puja space and altar, perform the Abhishek of the deity.

nodes.label: Dhyana
nodes.gloss: Puja should begin with the meditation of Lord Krishna . Dhyana should be done in front of already installed Lord Krishna statue in front of you. Following Mantra should be chanted while meditating on Lord Shri Krishna.

nodes.label: Avahana
nodes.gloss: After Dhyana of Lord Krishna, one should chant following Mantra in front of the Murti, by showing Aavahan Mudra (Aavahan Mudra is formed by joining both palms and folding both thumbs inwards).

nodes.label: Asana
nodes.gloss: After Lord Krishna has been invoked, take five flowers in Anjali (by joining palm of both hands) and leave them in front of the Murti to offer seat to Shri Krishna while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Padya
nodes.gloss: After offering seat to Lord Krishna offer Him water to wash the feet while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Arghya
nodes.gloss: After Padya offering, offer water to Shri Krishna for head Abhishekam while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Achamana
nodes.gloss: After Arghya offering, offer water to Shri Krishna for Achamana (water for sipping) while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Snana
nodes.gloss: After Achamana, offer water to Shri Krishna for the bath while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Vastra
nodes.gloss: Wear Lord Krishna in bright silk clothes and adorn him with jewellery.

nodes.label: Yajnopavita
nodes.gloss: After Vastra offering, offer Yajnopavita to Shri Krishna while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Gandha
nodes.gloss: After Yajnopavita offering, offer scent to Shri Krishna while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Pushpa
nodes.gloss: Now offer various fragrance stuffs to Lord Krishna while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Dhupa
nodes.gloss: Now offer Dhupa to Shri Krishna while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Dipa
nodes.gloss: Now offer Deep to Shri Krishna while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Naivedya
nodes.gloss: Offer fresh butter, fruits, and Chhappan Bhog in front of him.

nodes.label: Tambula
nodes.gloss: Now offer Tambula (Paan with betel nuts) to Shri Krishna while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Namaskara
nodes.gloss: Now offer symbolic Pradakshina (circumambulate from left to right of Shri Krishna) with flowers while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Aarti
nodes.gloss: To perform the sacred day, it starts with fasting until the stroke of midnight. Embrace it with a small swing and prepare a bath of honey, milk, and curd for the lord.

nodes.label: Kshama Prarthana
nodes.gloss: After Namaskar, seek pardon from Shri Krishna for any known-unknown mistakes done during Puja while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.title: Krishna Janmashtami Nana Parimala Dravya Mantra

nodes.label: Dhyana
nodes.gloss: First of all one should meditate and remember Shri Kuber with following Mantra (Shri Kubera Dhyana Mantra).

nodes.label: Avahana
nodes.gloss: After Dhyanam of Shri Kuber, invoke Him with following Mantra. While calling Shri Kuber your hands should be in Aavahan Mudra in front of the chest (Aavahan Mudra is formed by joining both palms and folding both thumbs inwards).

nodes.label: Gandha
nodes.gloss: After this perform Shri Kuber Puja with Chandan, Akshata, Pushpa, Doop, Deep and Naivedhya while chanting following Mantras.

nodes.label: Pushpa
nodes.gloss: Once Shri Kuber has come to the chest, take five flowers in Anjali (by joining palm of both hands) and leave them in front of the chest to offer the seat to Shri Kuber while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Mantra Pushpanjali
nodes.gloss: Once Shri Kuber has come to the chest, take five flowers in Anjali (by joining palm of both hands) and leave them in front of the chest to offer the seat to Shri Kuber while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.title: Shri Kubera Nav Upchara Pujan Mantra (Devanagari)
Shri Kubera Nav Upchara Pujan Mantra (Roman)

nodes.label: Shuddhi / Cleaning
nodes.gloss:
Wake up early in the morning, clean the surroundings and space where pooja is to be performed. Also, sprinkle Gangajaal to purify the house. Cleanliness not only drives away the negativity but is also liked by Goddess Lakshmi and is said to visit only those houses that are always clean.
Regarded as a feminine and sacred utility in the Hindu culture, the ‘broom’ is honored by offering Haldi-Kumkum. Often considered as Mother Lakshmi- The Goddess of Wealth, a broom is given high respect and which is why touching the broom by foot is a reflection of disrespect towards Maa Lakshmi.
Once done with the cleaning process, take a bath, and complete the self-purification or Atma Shodhan process. An auspicious bath before sunrise not only cleans one’s body but also destroys the Raja-Tama components, keeps the soul focused and conscious of performing any puja Vidhi. Promising to perform the puja Vidhi with great dedication, you have to make Sankalpana by taking water in your fist.

nodes.label: Achamana (self-purification)
nodes.gloss: Aachaman (आचमन)

nodes.label: Ganesha Puja / Smarana
nodes.gloss: Just before commencing the puja Vidhi, worship Lord Ganesha. Also known as “Vighnaharta,” Ganpati Bappa is said to remove the obstacles, or Vighna is one’s path. Worshiping Lord Kubera, the treasurer of all gods, brings prosperity, good fortune, and wealth. Also, the three famous forms of Goddess Lakshmi are worshipped on this day. Maa Mahasaraswati; The Goddess of Learning and Books, is honored to seek knowledge and other musical skills. ‘The Goddess of Wealth and Money’- Mahalakshmi is worshiped for happiness, courage. Goddess Mahakali, when worshipped with complete devotion, helps you achieve your aspirations and dreams.

nodes.label: Kalasha Sthapana
nodes.gloss: Just before placing a Kalash, spread a heap of rice grains in the center of the chowki, which acts as an Asana. Apply Haldi-kumkum to the Kalash and the idols. Fill the silver or brass Kalash with water, supari, coins, Akshat rice, marigold flower, a pinch of Haldi-Kumkum, and cover the mouth of the same with Mango leaves and a coconut.

nodes.label: Dhyana
nodes.gloss: Puja should begin with the meditation of Bhagawati Lakshmi. Dhyana should be done in front of already installed Shri Lakshmi statue in front of you. Following Mantra should be chanted while meditating on Bhagawati Shri Lakshmi.

nodes.label: Avahana
nodes.gloss: After Dhyan of Shri Bhagwati Lakshmi, one should chant following Mantra in front of the Murti, by showing Aavahan Mudra (Aavahan Mudra is formed by joining both palms and folding both thumbs inwards).

nodes.label: Asana
nodes.gloss: Now comes the installation of an altar or chowki where the puja is to be performed. Red is the color of purity, love, and Shakti, which is why you have to place a soft red cloth on the chowki. Then carefully place the idol of Maa Lakshmi along with the Lord Ganesha idol on her left side as she is his mother.

nodes.label: Padya
nodes.gloss: After welcoming Shri Lakshmi offer Her water to wash the feet while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Arghya
nodes.gloss: After Padya offering, offer water to Shri Lakshmi for head Abhishekam while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Achamana
nodes.gloss: Now offer water to Shri Lakshmi for Achamana while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Snana
nodes.gloss: After Arghya offering, offer water to Shri Lakshmi for the bath while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Vastra
nodes.gloss: Now offer Moli (मोली) as new clothes to Shri Lakshmi while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Gandha
nodes.gloss: Now offer scent bath to Shri Lakshmi while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Pushpa
nodes.gloss: After Shri Lakshmi has been invoked, take five flowers in Anjali (by joining palm of both hands) and leave them in front of the Murti to offer seat to Shri Lakshmi while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Dhupa
nodes.gloss: Now offer honey and milk to Shri Lakshmi while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Dipa
nodes.gloss: Now offer Deep to Shri Lakshmi while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Naivedya
nodes.gloss: Now offer Naivedhya to Shri Lakshmi while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Tambula
nodes.gloss: Now offer Tambool (Paan with betel nuts) to Shri Lakshmi while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Namaskara
nodes.gloss: Now offer symbolic Pradakshina (circumambulate from left to right of Shri Lakshmi) with flowers while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Aarti
nodes.gloss: Preparation: Purify the puja place and yourself. Keep the lord idol on a wooden chowki covered with a red cloth.

nodes.label: Mantra Pushpanjali
nodes.gloss: After Shri Lakshmi has been invoked, take five flowers in Anjali (by joining palm of both hands) and leave them in front of the Murti to offer seat to Shri Lakshmi while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Kshama Prarthana
nodes.gloss: Now seek pardon from Shri Lakshmi for any known-unknown mistakes done during Puja while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Prasad Distribution
nodes.gloss: Distribute the prasad (food offering) among family members and devotees as a token of blessings from Lord Ganesh.

nodes.gloss: Lord Ganesha is sought first to clear the path of any problems according to Vedic rites.

nodes.label: Kalasha Sthapana
nodes.gloss: Place a copper pot filled with water and sacred herbs to show the divine presence of deities in the ritual.

nodes.label: Katha / Path
nodes.gloss: The ceremony reaches its final offering, indicating total surrender to the divine intention. Offering 16-Ahutis with Kheer, along with reciting the Sri suktam path, gives more benefits.

nodes.label: Havan / Homa
nodes.gloss: The sacred fire ritual is done using dry wood and ghee. Offerings of medicinal herbs, lotus seeds, and personalized woods are offered into the fire.

nodes.label: Snana
nodes.gloss: A holy bath is performed by continuously pouring water, milk, curd, or more over the Shiva Lingam while chanting certain mantras. After which, offerings of flowers, sweets, chandan, Bilva leaves, and incense sticks are made.

nodes.label: Pushpa
nodes.gloss: Now, pandits will begin rhythmic recitation of the Mahamrityunjaya Mantra. It is usually done 108 times, 11,000 times, or the complete 1.25 Lakh (Sava Lakh) times using Rudraksha mala.

nodes.label: Dhupa
nodes.gloss: Begin by sitting in a comfortable position facing east or north, as these directions are considered auspicious. Light a lamp and incense sticks to purify the atmosphere and create a conducive environment for the Jaap.

nodes.label: Dipa

nodes.label: Aarti

nodes.label: Sankalpa
nodes.gloss: After taking bath devotees should take Sankalp (संकल्प) to observe full day fast and to break the fast on the next day. During Sankalp devotees pledge for self-determination throughout the fasting period and seek blessing of Lord Shiva to finish the fast without any interference. Hindu fasts are strict and people pledge for self-determination and seek God blessing before starting them to finish them successfully.

nodes.label: Snana
nodes.gloss: On the day of Shivaratri, one should get up early in the morning and take bath. It is suggested to add black sesame seeds into the water. It is believed that the holy bath on the day of Shivaratri purifies not only the body but also the soul. If possible bathing in Ganges is preferred.

nodes.label: Gandha
nodes.gloss: After that Chandan or Kumkum is applied to the Shiva Linga which is followed by lighting lamp and Dhupa. The other items which are used to adorn Lord Shiva include flower of Madar (मदार) which is also known as Aak (आक), Vibhuti which is also known as Bhasm. Vibhuti is sacred ash which is made using dried cow dung.

nodes.label: Dhupa

nodes.gloss: A couple sits together, holding water and red flowers, making a formal vow in front of the deity to stay committed to each other and their spiritual way.

nodes.gloss: Do a Ganesh puja first to eliminate any problem coming in the puja. Perform this with abhishekam of the select deities through milk, honey, and curd.

nodes.gloss: Consult an expert to locate a good Muhurat called Shubh Muhurat. The ritual is best undertaken at a temple, at the bank of a holy river, or at home in a sanctified place. During the ritual, the child must face East.

nodes.label: Visarjana
nodes.gloss: The child is made to wash his head in Gangajal and apply a cooling paste of Sandalwood and Turmeric to heal the head and soothe the senses. The shaved hair is then gathered, combined with flour or cow dung, and then reverently placed in a running river (Visarjan).

nodes.label: Tambula

nodes.label: Aarti

nodes.gloss: Puja should begin with the meditation of Lord Satyanarayan. Dhyana should be done in front of Satyanarayana image or idol in front of you. Following Mantra should be chanted while meditating on Lord Satyanarayan.

nodes.label: Avahana
nodes.gloss: After Dhyana of Lord Satyanarayan, one should chant following Mantra in front of the Murti, by showing Avahan Mudra (Avahan Mudra is formed by joining both palms and folding both thumbs inwards).

nodes.label: Asana
nodes.gloss: After Lord Satyanarayana has been invoked, take five flowers in Anjali (by joining palm of both hands) and leave them in front of the Murti to offer seat to Lord Satyanarayana while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Padya

nodes.label: Arghya
nodes.gloss: After Padya offering, offer water to Shri Satyanarayana for head Abhishekam while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Achamana
nodes.gloss: After Arghya offering, offer water to Shri Satyanarayana for Achamana while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Snana
nodes.gloss: After Achamaniya offering, give a bath with Panchamrita (the mixture of milk, curd, honey, Ghee and sugar) to Shri Satyanarayana while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Vastra
nodes.gloss: After Shuddhodaka Snana, now offer new clothes to Shri Satyanarayana while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Yajnopavita
nodes.gloss: After Vastra offering, offer holy thread to Shri Satyanarayana while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Gandha

nodes.label: Dhupa

nodes.label: Dipa

nodes.label: Naivedya
nodes.gloss: After Deepam offering, wash your hands and offer Naivedya. It should include different type of fruits and sweets and offer this to Lord Satyanarayana while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Tambula

nodes.label: Namaskara
nodes.gloss: After Aarti, now offer symbolic Pradakshina (circumambulate from left to right of Shri Satyanarayan) with flowers in hand while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Aarti
nodes.gloss:
After offering fruits, offer Aarti with lit camphor in Puja Thali to Lord Satyanarayana while chanting following Mantra.
After chanting following Mantra sing Shri Satyanarayana Aarti in praise of Lord Satyanarayan.

nodes.label: Mantra Pushpanjali
nodes.gloss: After Pradakshina, offer incantations and flowers to Lord Satyanarayana while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Prasad Distribution
nodes.gloss: 6. Aarti & Prasad Distribution : To conclude, light a diya to perform the final aarti of Lord Satyanarayan and seek the blessings. Followed by these, distribute the parasad among all the participants.

nodes.label: Havan / Homa
nodes.gloss: A small Havan is performed, concluding with the Maha Aarti and Prasad distribution.

nodes.label: Sankalpa
nodes.gloss: The person performing the puja should hold water in his right palm and perform a Maha Sankalp or Vachanam, in which they should beg the Navagrahas for their blessings and for them to grant their wishes.

nodes.label: Ganesha Puja / Smarana
nodes.gloss: After the Ganesh Puja and Sthapana of the Navagraha idols, the Navagraha Puja begins.

nodes.label: Kalasha Sthapana
nodes.gloss: The first ritual step in the Navagraha Puja calls for Kalash Sthapana.

nodes.label: Navagraha / attendant deities
nodes.gloss: Call upon the Navagrahas, or the nine planets, before beginning the Puja.

nodes.label: Avahana
nodes.gloss: For the Sankshipt Navagraha Puja of the nine planets, place a vessel or earthen cup in front of the Kalasha. Ganapati Puja: The Ganapati Puja is typically performed first, as Lord Ganesha is the remover of obstacles; offer flowers, chant the Ganapati mantra, and perform the Aarti, offering Durva grass and Modak to please Lord Ganesha. Havan (Fire Ritual): If you are conducting a Havan, you will offer ghee, grains, and other sacred items to the fire while chanting specific mantras for prosperity, protection, and success. Aarti (Prayers): The final ritual involves Aarti, where you wave a lit lamp or camphor in front of the deities while chanting prayers, and afterwards seek the blessings of all present by offering prasad to everyone.

Collect all the essential materials, such as: Kalash (sacred pot) filled with water, mango leaves, and a coconut on top; fresh flowers (particularly marigold), fruits, and sweets (such as ladoos or peda); Panchamrit for the abhishek (milk, curd, honey, ghee, and sugar); Roli, sindoor, akshat, and holy thread (moli); incense sticks and diya for lighting; coins for prosperity; picture or idol of Lord Ganesha and other deities like Goddess Lakshmi and Lord Kuber. Place the Kalash in the center, surrounding it with flowers, fruits, sweets, and the puja materials in a systematic manner. If performing a Havan, set up a yajna kund (fire altar) safely.

Decorate the Space: To invoke positive energy, decorate the area with fresh flowers, traditional Rangoli designs, and lighting; you can also use torans (door hangings) made of marigold flowers. Create a Puja Platform or Table: Set up a small platform or table where the puja will be performed, covered with a clean cloth, preferably red or white. Ensure Ventilation: Light incense sticks (dhoop) and diyas to purify the air and create a positive environment.

nodes.gloss: After Padya offering, offer water to Shri Satyanarayana for head Abhishekam while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Achamana
nodes.gloss: After Arghya offering, offer water to Shri Satyanarayana for Achamana while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Snana
nodes.gloss: One should take a bath in the evening on the day of Purnima and Sankranti.

nodes.label: Vastra
nodes.gloss: After Shuddhodaka Snana, now offer new clothes to Shri Satyanarayana while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Yajnopavita
nodes.gloss: After Vastra offering, offer holy thread to Shri Satyanarayana while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Gandha
nodes.gloss: After Yajnopavita offering, offer sandalwood paste or powder to Shri Satyanarayana while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Pushpa
nodes.gloss: After Chandan offering, offer flowers to Shri Satyanarayana while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Dhupa
nodes.gloss: After Pushpa offering, offer Dhupa to Shri Satyanarayana while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Dipa
nodes.gloss: After Dhupam offering, offer enlightened earthen lamp of pure Ghee to Lord Satyanarayana while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Naivedya
nodes.gloss: After Deepam offering, wash your hands and offer Naivedya. It should include different type of fruits and sweets and offer this to Lord Satyanarayana while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Tambula
nodes.gloss: After offering Naivedyam, offer betel leaf (Tambula) to Lord Satyanarayana while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Namaskara
nodes.gloss: After Aarti, now offer symbolic Pradakshina (circumambulate from left to right of Shri Satyanarayan) with flowers in hand while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Katha / Path
nodes.gloss: After this read or listen to the story of Lord Satyanarayana Vrat Katha.

nodes.label: Aarti
nodes.gloss: After offering fruits, offer Aarti with lit camphor in Puja Thali to Lord Satyanarayana while chanting following Mantra. After chanting following Mantra sing Shri Satyanarayana Aarti in praise of Lord Satyanarayan.

nodes.label: Mantra Pushpanjali
nodes.gloss: After Pradakshina, offer incantations and flowers to Lord Satyanarayana while chanting following Mantra.

nodes.label: Prasad Distribution
nodes.gloss: Light a diya to perform the final aarti of Lord Satyanarayan, seek his blessings, and then distribute the prasad among all participants.

nodes.label: Havan / Homa
nodes.gloss: A small Havan is performed, concluding with the Maha Aarti and Prasad distribution.

nodes.label: Snana
nodes.gloss: Keep the lord idol or a Shani yantra in a clean place. Perform Chhaya daan by looking at your reflection in a bowl of mustard oil. Offer that oil to the lord. It shows the surrendering of your shadow or negative abilities.

nodes.label: Pushpa
nodes.gloss: Light a lamp using mustard oil. Present black sesame seeds, whole black gram, and blue flowers. The particular offerings are thought to absorb the intense energy of Saturn and provide relief to the performer.

nodes.label: Aarti
nodes.gloss: Wake up early in the morning and take a bath. Multiple people keep fasting from Saturday sunrise to sunset. Wear clean, dark colored clothes to cater to the deity's preferred colours.

nodes.name: Shiv Puran
nodes.source_note: single-source extraction — pending corroboration

nodes.label: Avahana
nodes.gloss: Begin with a prayer to Lord Ganesha to eliminate obstacles, then invoke Lord Shiva's blessings with mantras like "Om Namah Shivaya."

nodes.label: Snana
nodes.gloss: Bathe the Shivlinga with water, milk, honey, curd, and ghee while reciting Shiva mantras.

nodes.label: Gandha
nodes.gloss: Prepare puja items including flowers, incense sticks, diya, fruits, milk, honey, water, sandalwood paste, and a copy of the Shiv Puran.

nodes.label: Pushpa
nodes.gloss: Present bel leaves, flowers, and fruits while lighting incense sticks with devotion and focus.

nodes.label: Dhupa
nodes.gloss: Present bel leaves, flowers, and fruits while lighting incense sticks with devotion and focus.

nodes.label: Aarti
nodes.gloss: Start with a sacred bath and clean, white or saffron-colored clothes. Set up a clean altar with an image of Lord Shiva, along with a Shivlinga if possible.

nodes.name: Shuddhikaran
nodes.source_note: single-source extraction — pending corroboration

nodes.label: Sankalpa
nodes.gloss: The devotee expresses their intention for purification.

nodes.label: Kalasha Sthapana
nodes.gloss: The spray of energized Kalash water (Teertha) throughout the complete area to fill positive energy.

nodes.label: Havan / Homa
nodes.gloss: Herbs and ghee are presented into a consecrated fire, emitting smoke that serves as a powerful environment cleanser.

nodes.name: Sunderkand
nodes.source_note: single-source extraction — pending corroboration

nodes.label: Sankalpa
nodes.gloss: Sankalp taken in your name & gotra.

nodes.label: Aarti
nodes.gloss: Prasad & Aarti at ceremony conclusion.

nodes.name: Teharavi
nodes.source_note: single-source extraction — pending corroboration

nodes.label: Shuddhi / Cleaning
nodes.gloss: The day starts with a complete cleaning of the house. This may involve a Havan (sacred fire ceremony) and sprinkling of holy water to eliminate the Sutak (period of impurity) with death.

nodes.label: Vastra
nodes.gloss: Families express their gratitude by giving the deceased important things like clothes, umbrellas, lamps, or even grain (Sajja Daan) to make his or her life in the afterlife more comfortable.

nodes.label: Katha / Path
nodes.gloss: The ceremony is completed with a group prayer of peace, and friends and extended family come to offer their final goodbyes, and the formal mourning period is over.

nodes.name: Tripindi Shradh
nodes.source_note: single-source extraction — pending corroboration

nodes.label: Shuddhi / Cleaning
nodes.gloss: The ritual begins with a holy dip in sacred rivers to purify the body and mind. Then, devotees take a vow to perform the rite with devotion for the ancestors' peace.

nodes.label: Kalasha Sthapana
nodes.gloss: A sacred pot is placed to invoke Lord Brahma, Lord Vishnu, and Lord Shiva, as they are presiding deities.

nodes.label: Arghya
nodes.gloss: Water with black sesame is presented while the pandit chants a clear mantra to quench the thirst of the forefathers.

nodes.label: Tambula
nodes.gloss: The ritual concludes with offering food, clothes, or dakshina to Brahmins, which is believed to reach directly to ancestors. It completes the cycle of Karma healing.

nodes.label: Havan / Homa
nodes.gloss: A fire is lit up in the havan khund, and items like ghee and holy herbs are offered while chanting a mantra to seek peace for the departed soul.

nodes.name: Udaka Shanti
nodes.source_note: single-source extraction — pending corroboration

nodes.label: Sankalpa
nodes.gloss: Start the puja by mentioning your name, gotra, and the objective of performing the puja in the presence of a qualified, trained pandit. The declaration caters to your purpose with the ritual.

nodes.label: Ganesha Puja / Smarana
nodes.gloss: Honour Lord Ganesh to eliminate problems. Offer flowers, incense, and sweets to receive his blessings and assure the puja is complete without any disturbance.

nodes.label: Kalasha Sthapana
nodes.gloss: Fill a copper or clay pot with sacred water. Keep mango leaves on top of it and coconut. Invoke all holy rivers into this water with specific mantras, making it a spiritual centre of the puja.

nodes.label: Aarti
nodes.gloss: The pandit then sprinkles the cleansed water on all participants using a bundle of mango leaves or darbha grass. It purifies every corner of negativity and brings peace.

nodes.name: Uma Maheshwari
nodes.source_note: single-source extraction — pending corroboration

nodes.label: Sankalpa
nodes.gloss: Clean and purify the puja altar. Keep an image of Shiv and Parvati together on a yellow cloth. Start with sankalp: hold water in your palm, and state your name and goal to the lord.

nodes.label: Dhyana
nodes.gloss: Offer them fruits, sweets, and incense sticks. Chant the Uma Maheshwari stotram or meditate silently on the divine couple's unified form to channel your energy with theirs.

nodes.label: Avahana
nodes.gloss: Begin by pleasing Lord Ganesh, offering flowers and dhoop batti to ensure the puja remains obstacle-free.

nodes.label: Snana
nodes.gloss: Bathe the idols with water, milk, and honey gently while reciting 'Om namah shivaya', and 'Om umaye namah'. Embellish them with sandalwood paste, fresh bilva leaves, and vermilion.

nodes.label: Aarti
nodes.gloss: Clean and purify the puja altar. Keep an image of Shiv and Parvati together on a yellow cloth. Start with sankalp: hold water in your palm, and state your name and goal to the lord.

nodes.name: Upanayan Sanskar
nodes.source_note: single-source extraction — pending corroboration

nodes.label: Shuddhi / Cleaning
nodes.gloss: The morning starts with the purification ceremony, Ganesh Puja, and a special bath for the child to signify the shedding of his previous uninitiated status.

nodes.label: Dhyana
nodes.gloss: The child learns the prayers and meditation he must do each day to keep the thread pure and to concentrate his mind on his studies.

nodes.name: Varshika Shraddha
nodes.source_note: single-source extraction — pending corroboration

nodes.label: Sankalpa
nodes.gloss: The individual starts performing the ritual by taking a holy vow, mentioning their name, gotra, and the goal of the ceremony to seek the presence of ancestors.

nodes.label: Sankalpa
nodes.gloss: Start the Puja by making a promise. Say your name and pray for the health of your loved ones.

nodes.label: Ganesha Puja / Smarana
nodes.gloss: Lord Ganesha is offered prayers first to remove all problems, followed by the Navagraha (nine planetary) puja to reduce any adverse planetary effects impacting the home and its occupants.

nodes.label: Kalasha Sthapana
nodes.gloss: A Kalash filled with water set up as the ritual's key anchor. Mango leaves and coconut are placed on top of it. This makes it the living centre of ceremony.

nodes.label: Navagraha / attendant deities
nodes.gloss: Lord Ganesha is offered prayers first to remove all problems, followed by the Navagraha (nine planetary) puja to reduce any adverse planetary effects impacting the home and its occupants.

nodes.label: Pushpa
nodes.gloss: Pray to the spirit of the house. Use flowers, rice, and a small copper idol. This invites the power of the gods into the walls.

nodes.label: Havan / Homa
nodes.gloss: A Vedic Pandit will light the holy fire. The smoke from the fire cleans the entire house from within.

nodes.label: Muhurat selection
nodes.gloss: Consult a Hindu calendar (panchang) or a priest to choose an appropriate muhurat. Saturdays and Tuesdays are not preferred, and Wednesday and Thursday mornings are favourable.

nodes.label: Sankalpa
nodes.gloss: The head of the family or a priest starts with Sankalp, after which Ganesha is invoked. The vehicle will have a kumkum and turmeric tilak on its front or bonnet. The coconut is smashed in front of the car as a representation of eliminating barriers and bad vibes.

nodes.label: Pushpa
nodes.gloss: Wash the car well and adorn it with new marigold garlands, mango leaves, and colourful ribbons. A clean car is a symbol of respect and willingness to be blessed by the gods.

nodes.label: Aarti
nodes.gloss: Put a tiny idol or picture of Lord Ganesha by the car. Prepare necessary puja material: kumkum (vermilion), turmeric, rice grains (akshat), flowers, coconut, incense sticks, camphor, a diya, and betel leaves.

nodes.label: Prasad Distribution
nodes.gloss: The puja is completed with a lamp aarti that goes around the vehicle, and then prasad is distributed to everyone present.

nodes.name: Vishwakarma
nodes.source_note: single-source extraction — pending corroboration

nodes.label: Aarti
nodes.gloss: The devotees store the Vishwakarma idol and prepare for the puja overnight. Clean up all the equipment and take a seat facing east. Utter mantras. Start an aarti and light an akhanda diya.

nodes.label: Visarjana
nodes.gloss: The employees get the blessings of Lord Vishwakarma on the auspicious day of Vishwakarma Puja. The next morning of the puja, the idol of Lord Vishwakarma is immersed in the water.

nodes.name: Vyapar Vriddhi
nodes.source_note: single-source extraction — pending corroboration

nodes.label: Sankalpa
nodes.gloss: Light a Ghee lamp and incense sticks. In your right hand, hold some water, rice, and flowers, and make a Sankalpa (solemn vow), saying your name, your business name, and the specific goal that you plan to achieve, whether it is to clear your debt, increase your sales, or expand to new locations.

nodes.label: Snana
nodes.gloss: Take the Panchamrit Abhishek (holy bath) of Panchamrit, Yantra, and idols. Apply sandalwood paste and vermilion. Bring fresh flowers, sweets (Bhoga), and fruits of the season. Repeat your 'Vyapar Vriddhi Mantra' or the Sri Suktam with utmost concentration to lift the mood.

nodes.label: Aarti
nodes.gloss: Clean your office or shop. In the North-East corner, place a wooden platform (Chowki). Lay a fresh red cloth over it and put the idols of Lord Ganesha and Goddess Lakshmi, and a consecrated Vyapar Vriddhi Yantra.
